answer: The most famous couplet about hopelessness in Ghalib's 'Koi ummeed bar nahin aati' is the opening couplet (the matla), couplet 1: 'Koi ummeed bar nahin aati / Koi soorat nazar nahin aati' ('No hope I have is ever answered / No way ahead can be uncovered'). This is the defining statement of the ghazal — it gives the poem its title and directly articulates total hopelessness. Found in Diwan-E-Ghalib, chapter 115 (mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c), paragraph p-92c600.
